readme.purpose: CAPE (Cyclically Adjusted P/E) configuration for portfolio projection scenario bands. Used by the UI to compute deterministic scenario lines alongside Monte Carlo results.
readme.cape_current: Current Shiller CAPE ratio. Update periodically to reflect current market valuation. As of early 2026, CAPE ~35. Source: https://www.multpl.com/shiller-pe
readme.cape_historical_mean: Long-run historical CAPE mean (~17). Used as the 'optimistic' scenario — assumes valuations revert to mean. Do not change unless you have a strong view on the new normal.

adjustment_config.inflation_assumption: Annual inflation assumption used to convert CAPE-implied nominal return to real return. Should match core_inflation_pct in economicglobal.json.
adjustment_config.earnings_growth_assumption: Real earnings growth assumption. Historical average ~2%. Used in Gordon Growth Model: implied_return = earnings_yield + growth.

readme.how_cape_drives_bands: CAPE-implied 10yr real return = 1/CAPE - inflation. At CAPE 35: 1/35 = 2.86% nominal - 2.5% inflation = ~0.3% real. At historical mean CAPE 17: 1/17 = 5.9% - 2.5% = ~3.4% real. These become the pessimistic and optimistic scenario lines on the Portfolio Projection chart.
readme.update_frequency: Update cape_current quarterly or when CAPE moves more than 5 points. The scenario bands are illustrative — they show what a CAPE-mean-reversion scenario would look like vs pure Monte Carlo.
